mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS at 4.33%) signals sustained monetary tightening. Higher policy rates compress near-term bond valuations and increase real yields, driving 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 20 trading days as market reprices duration risk.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 10 basis points over 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.2% combined with payroll employment at 159,398 thousand indicates tight labor market. Persistent labor tightness sustains wage pressure and inflation expectations, forcing the Fed to maintain or raise rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.5% AND PAYEMS growth exceeds 100k per month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 15 basis points over 60 trading days despite trigger activation, or joint signal (low UNRATE + high PAYEMS) fails to predict higher yields in > 50% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 722,708 million) remain elevated, signaling robust consumer demand. Strong demand pressures inflation expectations and reduces Fed rate-cut probability, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise over the next 20 trading days as terminal rate expectations shift upward.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 8 basis points over 20 trading days when RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5%, or signal produces negative Rank IC over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO at 104.2062) shows supply-side resilience. Sustained supply capacity reduces stagflation risk and allows the Fed to maintain restrictive policy without economic collapse, supporting higher 10-year Treasury yields over the next 5 trading days as risk-off sentiment moderates.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 5 basis points over 5 trading days when INDPRO is above 104, or correlation between INDPRO growth and 5-day yield changes is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 1,501 thousand) combined with elevated retail sales (RSAFS) indicate broad-based demand strength. This dual demand signal compresses long-duration bond valuations and raises inflation expectations, driving 10-year Treasury yields downward as investors flee to safety and demand growth moderates over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 12 basis points over 20 trading days when both HOUST and RSAFS trigger conditions are met, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL at 319.615) remains elevated relative to historical averages. If inflation fails to decline further, the Fed signals extended rate maintenance, but market repricing of terminal rates lower as growth risks emerge, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall over the next 60 trading days as recession probability increases.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth falls below 0.2% AND FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days when trigger is active, or the interaction signal fails to predict lower yields in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.2% rises above 4.5% threshold, signaling labor market deterioration. Rising unemployment reduces inflation pressure and increases recession probability, prompting market expectations of Fed rate cuts, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall sharply over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and recession risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 10 basis points over 20 trading days following UNRATE breach above 4.5%, or signal produces negative Rank IC over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 23,542.349 billion chained 2017 dollars) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST below 1,400 thousand) signals demand deceleration. Slowing growth reduces inflation expectations and increases Fed rate-cut probability, driving 10-year Treasury yields downward over the next 60 trading days as terminal rate expectations compress.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days when HOUST falls below 1,400, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
